aliases: Al-Qaeda in the Lands of the Islamic Maghreb
Groupe Salafiste pour la Prédication et le Combat
Organization of al-Qa'eda in the Land of the Islamic Maghreb
Salafist Group for Preaching and Combat
description: Islamist militant organization active in North Africa, originating as the Salafist Group for Preaching and Combat (GSPC) in Algeria. It rebranded as AQIM in 2007 and is aligned with al-Qaeda. Engages in insurgency in the Maghreb and Sahel, funding itself largely through kidnapping for ransom. In 2017, its Sahara branch merged with other groups to form Jama'at Nasr al-Islam wal Muslimin.

country_links.evidence_quote: AQIM commander Talha claimed that his movement went to Niger, Algeria, Burkina Faso and Nigeria, to organize cells of AQIM.
country_links.context: AQIM has organized cells in Burkina Faso.

country_links.evidence_quote: 20 January 2019: AQIM claims the attack on 10 UN Mali peacekeepers due to Chad 's restoration of relations with Israel.
country_links.context: AQIM claimed an attack on UN Mali peacekeepers due to Chad's restoration of relations with Israel.

country_links.evidence_quote: attacks like the 2016 Grand-Bassam shootings in Ivory Coast being carried out by black AQIM members.
country_links.context: AQIM carried out the 2016 Grand-Bassam shootings in Ivory Coast.

country_links.evidence_quote: Yahia Djouadi, AQIM's leader for financing and logistics and former 'emir' of the group's Libyan operations.
country_links.context: AQIM has operations in Libya, including a former 'emir' of its Libyan operations.

country_links.evidence_quote: Membership is mostly drawn from the Algerian and local Saharan communities (such as the Tuaregs and Berabiche tribal clans of Mali)...
country_links.context: AQIM draws membership from local Saharan communities such as the Tuaregs and Berabiche tribal clans of Mali, and has carried out attacks and operations there.

country_links.evidence_quote: Hammam played a key role in perpetuating AQIM's terrorist activities in West Africa and Mali, and participated in several AQIM terrorist attacks in Mauritania.
country_links.context: AQIM has carried out terrorist attacks in Mauritania.

country_links.evidence_quote: as well as Moroccans from city suburbs of the North African country.
country_links.context: AQIM draws membership from Moroccans from city suburbs of the North African country.

country_links.evidence_quote: In December 2008: Two Canadian diplomats were taken hostage along with their driver in south-western Niger... The kidnappings were attributed to Al-Qaeda in the Islamic Maghreb.
country_links.context: AQIM has kidnapped hostages in Niger and claimed responsibility for attacks there.

country_links.evidence_quote: AQIM commander Talha claimed that his movement went to Niger, Algeria, Burkina Faso and Nigeria, to organize cells of AQIM.
country_links.context: AQIM has organized cells in Nigeria.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Uqba bin Nafi Battalion, the Tunisian wing of AQIM, claimed responsibility for an attack which killed six Tunisian policemen in Ghardimaou...
country_links.context: AQIM's Uqba bin Nafi Battalion, the Tunisian wing, claimed responsibility for attacks in Tunisia.
